title: HTML Image Extractor
shortDescription: Extract image sources, alt text, and dimensions from HTML.
metaDescription: Extract image sources, alt text, width, and height from HTML directly in your browser.
intro: Paste HTML to extract every img tag with its source, alt text, and dimensions in a structured list.
overview:
This tool reads HTML and pulls out image elements into a clean result you can inspect or copy. Each entry can include the image source, alt text, width, height, and any extra attributes present in the tag, which makes it useful when you need to review image markup precisely.

It is especially helpful when image data is buried inside exported templates, CMS output, or long snippets with mixed content. Because parsing happens locally in the browser, you can work with private markup without sending it elsewhere.
howItWorks: Paste your HTML into the input field.
The parser scans the markup for img elements.
It extracts src, alt, width, height, and related attributes when present.
Review the list and copy the extracted image data if needed.

useCases.title: Review missing alt text
useCases.description: Check a page export for image tags that do not include useful alt values before content goes live.

useCases.title: Collect image URLs from templates
useCases.description: Pull every image source from a newsletter or CMS fragment into a simple list for reuse or validation.

useCases.title: Verify declared dimensions
useCases.description: Inspect width and height values to spot images that may be stretched, swapped, or incorrectly sized.

useCases.title: Inspect lazy-loaded markup
useCases.description: Find image references stored in attributes such as data-src or similar template patterns.

useCases.title: Extract images from mixed HTML
useCases.description: Separate image data from long snippets that also contain links, text, and layout markup.

examples.title: Single image tag
examples.output: 1) src: /assets/hero.jpg | alt: City skyline at dusk | width: 1440 | height: 900
examples.description: A standard img element is parsed into separate fields.

examples.title: Image without explicit width
examples.output: 1) src: product.png | alt: Product front view
examples.description: Only the attributes that exist in the tag are returned.

examples.title: Template image with data attribute
examples.output: 1) data-src: /img/gallery-03.jpg | alt: Gallery item 3 | loading: lazy
examples.description: Useful for markup that stores the image path in a data attribute.

faq.question: Does this extractor read only img tags?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on HTML img elements and does not inspect CSS background images or images loaded through scripts.

faq.question: What happens if an image tag is incomplete?
faq.answer: The tool returns the attributes it can find, so a tag with only src and alt will produce a shorter result than a full tag.

faq.question: Can I use it on a fragment with many repeated images?
faq.answer: Yes. Every matching img element is extracted separately, even if several tags use the same source.

faq.question: Why do I sometimes see data-src instead of src?
faq.answer: Some templates store the real image path in data-src, data-original, or a similar attribute. The extractor can surface those values when they appear in the markup.

faq.question: Does it change or fix the HTML?
faq.answer: No. It only reads the markup and reports image-related attributes. It does not rewrite tags or guess missing values.

seo.title: HTML Image Extractor
seo.description: Extract image sources, alt text, width, and height from HTML directly in your browser.
